reviews: Coming to Clementi for so many times, I wasn’t aware of the existence of this food centre... Wanton Noodles... spicy and flavorful sauce...
Old School Wanton Mee... Sauce was spicy and made a great flavourful... spinach green noodles...
Spinach green noodles for wanton mee, sliced char siew, tasty wanton...
Some gems found in this forgotten part of clementi... wanton mee...
Prawn Dumpling Soup @ Hong Thye Noodles 福泰面家, Blk 353 Clementi Avenue 2 Cooked Food Centre #01-61.

reviews.author: C S
reviews.text: Bumped here to try their wanton mee! Yummilicious mee kia texture complemented with well marinated char siew. The wantons are wrapped up like siew mai. Will be back to try out their dumplings soup soon. 😋😋😋

reviews.author: Sharon
reviews.text:
No frills, old school wanton mee.
Enjoyable but nothing too remarkable.
I don't usually enjoy the char siews that are served with typical wanton mee because they tend to be dry and lean in texture. However, the char siew here is tender and well marinated.

Portion a bit small to be filling so top up needed if you are not a small eater.

reviews.author: Paulyn L
reviews.text: Best shrimp dumpling noodles! Huge dumplings with black fugus within. Noodles were smooth and q (not mashy kind). Old school flavourful taste. Very friendly elderly couple.

reviews.author: Sean Goh
reviews.text:
Happened to be in Clementi, decided to drop by the hawker centre at blk353 Clementi. Came here previously to try this stall but they were already closed, instead i tried another wanton noodle stall from the same row. Came here this time round at a much earlier timing. When i reached, there were a couple of people in the queue. Price for a bowl of wanton noodle starts from SGD$3.50 onward. This stall is operated by an elderly couple, they do accept cashless payment.

Ordered the wanton noodle which was SGD$3.50. Noodle was nicely cooked, texture of the noodle was springy. The char siew and wanton was pretty decent. Sauce slightly on the sweet side but stil acceptable.

Overall, noodle was definitely above average. Having tried both wanton noode stall in this hawker centre, i actually preferred this over the another one.

reviews.author: AC Chan
reviews.text: This stall is located in a quiet part of the neighbourhood, away from the bustling Clementi town. Their plump and flavourful dumplings are filled with minced pork and prawns with an added delight from the black fungus and water chestnut which give a nice crunchiness. 😋

reviews: Jalilah Corner always has a steady, long queue during weekend breakfasts. Some of the popular dishes I noticed on the surrounding tables include Mee Siam, Mee Rebus, and Nasi Lemak—though the Nasi Lemak is only available on weekends. The fried chicken was absolutely delicious—super crispy and freshly fried.

The gravy of the Mee Rebus was thick and very aromatic as you can smell the different types of spicies used. You can tasted the sweetness of the sweet potatoes and spiciness of the lemongrass, ginger and chilli right after the first mouth.

other_info.notes: Nasi Lemak only available on weekends.

reviews.author: edtayjb
reviews.text: Jalilah's Corner - this Malay food stall in 353 Clementi Avenue 2 Food Centre is my favourite stall at the aforementioned hawker centre. This stall serves very good Malay food staples like nasi lemak, nasi rawon, mee rebus, mee siam, mee soto, lontong, lontong kering, tahu goreng, and gado gado - the last of which is my all-time favourite at this stall. The gado gado here is very good and done in an old-school way, and it comes with a very moreish creamy peanut sauce. And the mee rebus here is also done in an old-school manner. However, I highly recommend the gado gado here if you are a fan of it. Service at the stall is very nice - friendly and courteous. The dining ambience here is as good as it can be for a hawker centre.

reviews.author: Winston Ho
reviews.text:
This is a halal stall that sells a variety of Malay food. They sell Nasi lemak only during the weekends. I ordered one for takeaway.

I also ordered their lontong, which was big in portion and packed with ingredients. It was tasty and filling.

They also have Mee Soto, Mee rebus, Mee Siam available.

On weekdays, they have Nasi Rawon too.

The makcik whose caricature is on the signboard is friendly and always smiling when taking your order. The staff prepares customers orders quickly and efficiently so the queue moves fast.

Recommended! 😋👍🏻👍🏻👍🏻

reviews.author: Sulaiman Chelios
reviews.text:
You know you're onto something when there's a line even before the sun finishes clocking in. A ragtag mix of office workers, uncles in slippers, aunties with grocery bags, and someone in pajamas who clearly didn’t plan to be out this long. That kind of line doesn’t form for nothing. That kind of line whispers “worth it” to anyone paying attention.

Then the plate arrives. No frills, no garnish gymnastics, just old-school nasi lemak. A black plastic plate that doesn’t pretend to be artisanal. It’s here to get the job done.

The rice? Steamy and confident. Not fancy basmati or gimmicky coconut overkill, just the real deal. Fragrant enough to remind you where you are: a hawker centre, not some faux-heritage café charging $24 for nostalgia. This was $5.30. And while it won’t win any Michelin stars, it will win your heart over if you grew up around people who cooked with feeling, not ego.

Let’s talk about the plate. It’s got everything you need to start the day grounded: fragrant white rice, a full hard-boiled egg, crispy ikan bilis, cucumber slices, fried chicken wing, a bergedil, and a fish fillet that’s as golden as the morning light slanting across the yellow tabletop. No fanfare. No unnecessary frills. Just honest food, fried and plated with quiet dignity.

The chicken wing is exactly what it should be, crunchy on the outside, seasoned enough to remind you this isn’t your first plate of nasi lemak, and it damn sure won’t be your last. The bergedil, soft and warm in the middle, holds together like a good secret. The fish fillet? Crunchy, flaky, not trying too hard, just enough batter, just enough salt.

The sambal… now this is what holds the plate together. It’s not some tourist-friendly sugar paste. It’s smoky, just a little sticky, and unapologetically bold. Not volcanic, but it's got character. The kind that tells you it's been made by someone who's been doing this for a long time.

And speaking of Puan Jalilah. The stall bears her name. An older woman behind the counter, kind-eyed, soft-spoken, and instantly familiar in that way only grandmothers can be. Watching her hand you your plate with steady hands, it hits you. This isn’t just breakfast. This is memory on a plate. You don’t just taste the food. You feel the care.

She reminds you of someone. Your someone. Maybe a late grandmother who used to fry anchovies just like that. Who never measured ingredients but always got it right. That gentle familiarity hits you somewhere deeper than hunger.

Yes, it was $5.30 but what you're paying for isn’t just food. It’s time, it’s memory, it’s warmth served on a black plate under fluorescent lights, with a line of people stretching out in quiet agreement: this is worth it.

And it was.

reviews.author: LP Pua
reviews.text: I have tried the Mee Siam at the other Malay stall in the same food centre and decided to try the Mee Siam at this stall for comparison. The Mee Siam was slightly sweet but retained the familiar traditional Mee Siam taste. I did not have a preference between the two stalls. The service was friendly. Note that two curry puffs for two dollars may seem expensive.

reviews.author: Winston Goh
reviews.text: Very affordably priced. Very yummy. Very satisfying. Will return for more. I will recommend this place to eat if you are at this food centre in Clementi Avenue 2 area 😃😋👍

reviews: The braised ducks that were on display looked damn good and I had to try it. The braised duck was tender, and portion was very big for a drumstick. The soup was herbalicious.

Tried their Braised Duck Rice set for $4.50 and find it reasonable in view of the generous portion of Duck Meat, Tofu, Salted Vege and Egg. A savoury sauce was added to both the ingredients and Yam Rice to intensify the taste.

reviews.author: S L
reviews.text: I was a regular there when they were at the coffee shop next to Kim San Leng. I believe many didn't know that they had moved to Blk 257 Food Hub. I tried their braised duck noodle 2 days ago; it was just as good as before but a little salty for me. Today, I tried their Kway Chap - the standard had dropped a little, but overall, it was still good. Unfortunately, I forgot to take a picture of the duck noodles 2 days ago and the Kway Chap just now - realised it only after I ate it. I went back for the duck noodles again and took a picture of it.

reviews.author: Alvin Lim
reviews.text: Duck noodles is saucy and tasty.I Like the noodles texture, soup taste blend initially, but it's actually quite nice.. Meats are tender and taste nice. Not spicy, recommended if you prefer sweet tasting duck noodles.

reviews.author: Han Yu Tan
reviews.text: I got the braised duck leg noodle which costs $6.5. I also added on an egg, some pork belly slices, and tofu for 50c each. The duck noodles are really good, very well flavoured, not too salty but still brimming with flavour. It’s just a bit on the oilier side which you can wash down with the soup given. The pork belly slices are really good, very tender and nice flavour. I would really recommend the tofu as it’s just tofu, but if you like it you can try.

reviews.author: Chong How
reviews.text: Kway is in a huge bowl, soup is warm n nice, not too salty. Ingredients such as eggs n pig skin, intestines I wld like the portion to be bigger, but nonetheless the taste is up to expectations. This portion shown is $5.

reviews.author: MNG
reviews.text:
Though located in a relatively obscured part of Bukit Panjang, it is a well known braised duck stall.

It offers rice, noodle and porridge options.

Kway teow is most traditional type which is thicker, giving it more chew and rice flavor. Mixed with its braising and chili sauces, it is a potent combination.

The rice had a savory soy sauce mix.

Braised duck was moderately braised with a good flavor and texture.

Its pricing is attractive and geared towards working crowd.

Highly recommended.

reviews: Tasty plate of Nasi Lemak with signature crispy deep fried chicken wing, sausage and egg omelette only for $3.50 Fluffy rice combining the classic chilli sauce and crispy anchovies. Yummy!

reviews.author: Sena Pathy
reviews.text: Hi happened on 26/02/2026 around 9 am. It was my worst experience ever buying nasi lemak from this shop. NASI LEMAK WITH NO IKAN BILLIS OR EGG!! $4.80 you ok or not sister? Nasi lemak without egg and ikan billis.. worst ever i eaten! Bodoh or what.. what service is this.. Deceive customers money with your nasi lemak.. Jai hind avoid if you can!

reviews.author: Henry Ho Si Tun
reviews.text: Ordered their Nasi Lemak. 3.5/5. Too much flour on the chicken wing.

reviews.author: LP Pua
reviews.text: There are a few types of Mee Siam, and even among traditional Malay stalls, there are many variations. Some are too sweet, which I don’t enjoy, while others are overly sour. This stall had just the right level of sourness, which suited my taste well. Priced at $4 for a big bowl, it exceeded expectations for the price point. I also bought a $1 curry puff, but it felt too modern for me. I prefer the traditional, small, and wrinkled type.

reviews.author: Ross Kanagawa
reviews.text: The food was ok ,but customer service could be better. And was not told about any surcharge or display any signage extra surcharges for CNY .

reviews.author: c tzehow
reviews.text: Delicious nasi lemak and nasi sambal goreng ayam. The chilli is so good, fried chicken is crispy and we like the bergedil.

cuisine_type: Hong Kong Cheong Fun / Dim Sum
reviews: Char Siew Cheong Fun is the classic combination and their best-seller. The silky smooth Cheong Fun is rather thin, springy and melt right in your mouth. You can taste its savoury sweet flavour of their Char Siew as they are seasoned well.

other_info.notes: Char Siew Cheong Fun is their best-seller.

reviews.author: Pretty Catmint
reviews.text: Bought their food twice and both times happy with my purchases. Tried their mushroom cheong fun, char siew cheong fun and pan-fried carrot cake and all were fresh, tasty and dim sum restaurant standard. The cheong funs are made fresh in front of you and contain enough fillings. They have sweet chili sauce and Hong Kong style savoury sambal and I opted for the latter. The cheong funs were topped with a light, sweet savoury soy sauce that suited the dishes well. Takeaway charges can come up to quite a bit if you buy a lot. Great that I no longer have to travel far for good Hong Kong style cheong fun.

reviews.author: Kim Tan
reviews.text: The lady making the food really take care of customers needs. She explained patiently the price information. Cheong Fun, Yam Cake and Fried Springrolls are really tasty!

reviews.author: A M
reviews.text: All I can say is… restaurant quality dim sum at food court!!

reviews.author: Kewis Qin
reviews.text: nice choose for dinner， traditional Gongdong food

reviews.author: Ian Wui
reviews.text: Fried prawn dumpling pathetically small. Taste wasn't good. Definitely needs alot of improvement.
